summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a tight 1966 show with standout Pigpen moments and a smoking Midnight Hour jam
review: The recording is a solid sound‑board source that, despite occasional clipping and faint white‑noise, delivers clear and lively audio. Musically the performance ranks among the best of ’66 – tight playing, strong vocals, a monster “Midnight Hour”, an inspiring “Dancing in the Streets” jam and early Rider’s, with Jerry Garcia’s solo work highlighted throughout.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviewers gave 5‑star ratings, praising both the musical performance and the audio quality; minor technical quirks keep it just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and lively sound; a few tracks are described as clipped or buzzy and there is faint white‑noise, but overall the audio is very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note a tight, well‑rehearsed set with strong vocals and cohesive transitions; the early Dead energy is captured throughout, making it one of the top ’66 performances.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s soloing is praised as great and “tearing it up”, especially on the Dancing in the Streets jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh is mentioned positively as part of the continuing “torch” of the band.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir (referred to as Bob) sounds good, contributing solid rhythm/vocals; his role in keeping the torch bright is highlighted.
